items.title: ERROR: Unable to identify distribution type (void)
items.author: Quin
items.content:
I’m attempting to install Crystal on Void Linux, but always get the following error:

ERROR: Unable to identify distribution type (void). You may specify a repository with the environment variable DISTRO_REPO

I installed 1.19.1 by creating ~/crystal and copying everything in there manually, but that doesn’t work very cleanly for updates, so I’d like to do this the right way. What’s needed here?

items.title: Crystal 1.20.2 released
items.author: /u/Meatack
items.content:
Small one today (May 15). Just 2 bug fixes from 1 contributor, but worth pulling if you're on the 1.20 line:

  • Backport of the randomness fix (#16909) - under certain conditions it could lose randomness over time. Already shipped to 1.19 back in 1.19.2; now landing on 1.20 too.

That's the whole release. No new features, no API changes - see the 1.20.0 notes if you missed the big stuff (new Process API preview, io_uring event loop, mold/lld defaults).

items.title: Crystal 1.20.2 is released!
items.author: Crys
items.content: We are announcing a new Crystal release 1.20.2 with a couple of regressions fixed.
